text.title: WebSocket Design Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the WebSocket Design methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Every message MUST use envelope `{v: int, type: string, id: string, ts: int, payload: object}`; raw payloads without envelope are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without `v`, breaking changes have no migration path; without `id`, retries and idempotency are impossible; without `ts`, ordering after reconnect is broken.

rule.statement: Both endpoints MUST send ping every 30s and disconnect after 90s of no pong; raw TCP keep-alive alone is insufficient.
rule.rationale: NAT and corporate firewalls drop idle TCP connections silently after 1-5 minutes; application-layer ping detects this faster than TCP keep-alive.

rule.statement: Client MUST reconnect with exponential backoff (base 1s, max 60s) + full jitter; fixed-interval reconnect is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Synchronised reconnects DoS the recovering server; jitter spreads the load over the window.

rule.statement: Server outgoing per-connection queue MUST be bounded (e.g. 1000 messages) with explicit shed policy (drop_oldest / drop_newest / close); unbounded queues are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Slow clients let unbounded queues grow unbounded; server OOMs while it still 'looks healthy'.

rule.statement: Handshake MUST authenticate via short-lived (≤5min) signed token in URL query or `Sec-WebSocket-Protocol` header; long-lived cookies as the only auth are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Long-lived cookies cannot be revoked promptly; short-lived tokens limit replay window.

rule.statement: Each message type MUST have a JSON schema; payload MUST be validated against the schema on both client and server; lax validation is forbidden.

rationale.source: JSON Schema usage; production fuzz retros

rule.rationale: Without schema validation, agents and malicious clients can crash handlers with malformed payloads.

rule.statement: Close MUST emit a code per RFC 6455 (1000 normal, 1001 going away, 1008 policy violation, 1009 too big, 4xxx app-specific); bare close without code is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without close codes, client cannot decide reconnect vs give-up; debugging is impossible.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
